zh.lifeSummary: 當William de Mandeville於1006年出生時，他的父親Geoffrey de Mandeville25歲，母親Mrs. Athelaise Maneville25歲。他和Mrs. William Maneville至少有1個兒子。他去世在英國米德萊可倫敦。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当William de Mandeville于1006年出生时，他的父亲Geoffrey de Mandeville25岁，母亲Mrs. Athelaise Maneville25岁。他和Mrs. William Maneville至少有1个儿子。他去世在英国米德萊可倫敦。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Mandeville
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Marcel, Andre, Armand, Gabrielle, Camille, Emile, Fernand, Fernande, Gaston, Jean-Paul, Lucien, Michel.

French: habitational name from Mandeville, the name of two places in Normandy, derived from Latin magna villa ‘large estate’, or a variant of Manneville, a habitational name from Manneville, the name of several places in Normandy (see 2 below). Compare Montville and Monville .
English (of Norman origin): habitational name from Manneville (Seine-Maritime, Calvados), Manneville-sur-Risle (Eure), or Magneville (Manche).

variants.name: Mansfield
meanings: English: habitational name from a place so named in Nottinghamshire. The placename means ‘open land by the river Maun’, from the river name Maun (which may take its name from a hill named with Old English mamme ‘teat’) + Old English feld ‘open country’.
Irish (Waterford): when not an importation of 1, this is an altered form of the Norman name de Manville (see Mandeville 3).
Americanized form of German and Jewish (Ashkenazic) Mansfeld, a habitational name from a place so called in Saxony.

variants.name: Manderville
meanings: English (of Norman origin): variant of   Mandeville  .

variants.name: Manwell
meanings: English and Scottish: variant of   Mandeville  .

variants.name: Manville
meanings: English (Sussex and Lancashire): variant of   Mandeville  .
Altered form of   Mainville   1, a surname of Swiss French origin (see   Miville  ).
French: variant of Menville, a habitational name from Menville in Haute-Garonne, and also of Mainville 2.

variants.name: Mundell
meanings: Scottish (of Norman origin): variant of   Mandeville  .
Americanized form of German Mündel (or some other similar, like-sounding surname): nickname for someone with a prominent mouth, from Middle High German munt, mündelīn ‘(little) mouth’.

variants.name: Monville
meanings: Altered form of French   Mandeville  .
Walloon: habitational name from Monville in Namur province in Wallonia (Belgium), of the same etymology as in 3 below.
In some cases possibly also French: habitational name from Montville in Seine-Maritime or Monville in Calvados, both named with Old French mont ‘mountain, hill’ + ville ‘settlement’.

variants.name: Mandal
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Chandi, Sanjay, Sankar, Somnath, Aditi, Aditya, Ajit, Amala, Amitabha, Anil, Anirudh, Aparna.
Indian (northern states): from Sanskrit maṇḍala, a word that has several meanings, including ‘sphere’ and ‘halo’.
English (Cumbria; of Norman origin): variant of   Mandeville  .

variants.name: Montville
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Emile, Vernice.
Altered form of French   Mandeville  .
French: variant of   Monville  . This surname is very rare in France, found mainly in Creuse.

variants.name: Manfull
meanings: English (Midlands): probably a variant of   Mandeville  . Compare   Manville  .

nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

historicEvents.title: Sweyn Forkbeard
historicEvents.description: The Danes raided and captured England. Sweyn, King of Danes, was declared King of England on Christmas Day.

historicEvents.title: Canut the Great
historicEvents.description: As Aethelred, King of England died, his son, Edmund Ironside devised a truce with Danish King Canute. The truce divided the kingdom between the two but Edmund died shortly after and became King of England. Several years later, he also became king of Denmark.

historicEvents.title: England Divided
historicEvents.description: King Canute died in 1035 and England was divided between his three sons. His son Sweyn received Norway, Harthacanute received Denmark, and Harold Harefoot received North England.

en.lifeSummary: When William de Mandeville was born in 1006, his father, Geoffrey de Mandeville, was 25 and his mother, Mrs. Athelaise Maneville, was 25. He had at least 1 son with Mrs. William Maneville. He died in London, Middlesex, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque William de Mandeville est né en 1006, son père, Geoffrey de Mandeville, avait 25 ans et sa mère, Mrs. Athelaise Maneville, avait 25 ans. Il a eu au moins 1 fils avec Mrs. William Maneville. Il est décédé à Londres, Middlesex, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als William de Mandeville 1006 geboren wurde, war sein Vater Geoffrey de Mandeville 25 und seine Mutter Mrs. Athelaise Maneville 25. Er hatte mit Mrs. William Maneville mindestens 1 Sohn. Er starb in London, Middlesex, England.

it.lifeSummary: Quando William de Mandeville è nato il nel 1006, il suo padre, Geoffrey de Mandeville, aveva 25 anni e il suo madre, Mrs. Athelaise Maneville, aveva 25 anni. Lui avuto almeno 1 figlio con Mrs. William Maneville. Morì nel London, Middlesex, England.

ja.lifeSummary: William de Mandevilleが1006年に生まれた時，父Geoffrey de Mandevilleは25歳，母Mrs. Athelaise Manevilleは25歳であった。彼はMrs. William Manevilleとの間に少なくとも1人の息子をもうけた。彼はイギリスミッドレセックスロンドンで死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: William de Mandeville(이)가 1006년에 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Geoffrey de Mandeville(은)는 25세였고, 그의 어머니 Mrs. Athelaise Maneville(은)는 25세였다. 그는 Mrs. William Maneville(와)과 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 영국 미들레세스 런던에서 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando William de Mandeville nasceu em 1006, seu pai, Geoffrey de Mandeville, tinha 25 anos e sua mãe, Mrs. Athelaise Maneville, tinha 25 anos. Ele teve pelo menos 1 filho com Mrs. William Maneville. Ele faleceu em London, Middlesex, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда William de Mandeville родился в 1006 г., его отцу по имени Geoffrey de Mandeville было 25 лет, а матери, которую звали Mrs. Athelaise Maneville, 25. У него был по крайней мере 1 сын от Mrs. William Maneville. Он умер в местности Лондон, миддлесекс, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando William de Mandeville nació en 1006, su papá, Geoffrey de Mandeville, tenía 25 años y su mamá, Mrs. Athelaise Maneville, tenía 25 años. Tuvo por lo menos 1 hijo con Mrs. William Maneville. Murió en London, Middlesex, Inglaterra.
